mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate earnings and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, or forward returns turn negative in >55% of low-inflation expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenue expectations, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of positive payroll revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0% signals restrictive monetary conditions that compress equity multiples and suppress quarterly market returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and stagnant housing starts indicates consumption-led expansion that outperforms construction-dependent sectors, boosting broad market returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% and HOUST month-over-month change < 0%
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by >0.5% or forward returns are negative in >55% of divergent growth scenarios.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during high unemployment periods signals severe output contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of high-unemployment contraction periods, or the signal shows no edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.
